data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rebounds as Oil Barrels Below $100; Trump's New Tariffs Kick In
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Friday alongside falling oil prices and a new wave of tariffs from President Trump.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brent crude (BZ) is back below $100 per barrel after briefly surpassing the key threshold on Thursday. The move helped ease long-term Treasury yields after they rose to an 18-month high earlier this week on oil-driven inflation concerns. That came after the Iran-backed Houthi military group attacked two Saudi tankers in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. 

However, U.S.-Iran tensions remain high. Trump said Thursday that he is considering a "massive attack" against Iran. Iran responded by saying that it is prepared to fully close the Bab al-Mandeb Strait and launch strikes on Tel Aviv if Trump follows through with the threat, two Iranian officials told the New York Post.

Meanwhile, a new round of U.S. tariffs took effect on Friday after Trump's sweeping 10% global tariffs expired. The previous tariffs were enforced under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which carry a maximum duration of 150 days without congressional approval. The new tariffs, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, have no expiration date or rate limit. Section 301 allows the president to impose tariffs in response to unfair foreign trade practices.

The U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) said the imports-produced-forced-labor">tariffs target forced labor practices. The duties apply to the 60 major U.S. trading partners and cover 99.4% of all imports. Countries that implement forced labor import bans will face a 10% tariff, while those that fail to implement such measures will face a 12.5% rate. China, Australia, and Egypt are among the countries facing the higher tariff, while the European Union, Mexico, and Indonesia will receive the lower rate.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.67% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Is Today's Dip a Buying Opportunity for Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) Stock?
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) stock took a beating on Friday, but that might not be a bad thing. With the stock's recent drop, this could represent a buying opportunity for traders who are interested in the digital health platform operator.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Checking out the latest analyst coverage of HIMS stock, the consensus rating for the shares is Moderate Buy, based on four Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average HIMS stock price target of $30.46. This represents a possible 4.27% upside for the shares.

While that average price target doesn't show much upside potential, new analyst coverage today suggests the stock still has room to grow. Citi analyst Daniel Grosslight increased his price target for HIMS stock to $35 from $28, which implies a possible 19.82% upside for the shares. However, the analysts stuck with a Hold rating for the stock.

Grosslight updated his coverage of HIMS stock as part of a wider update for the health technology sector ahead of Q2 earnings. He argued the higher price target for HIMS stock was warranted due to renewed confidence in value-based care enablers, turnaround execution and interest in growth assets.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market Week Ahead: Fed Rate Decision in Focus as Earnings Season Ramps Into Full Gear
data.description:
The trading week ended July 24 was another memorable stretch for investors as earnings season kicked off. In addition, Brent crude (BZ) briefly rallied above $100 per barrel, sending the 10-year Treasury yield to its highest level since January 2025. Investors also received a slate of earnings from Alphabet (GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), and Intel (INTC).

Earnings season is set to accelerate next week with several major tech companies on deck. Furthermore, the Fed will announce its interest rate decision on Wednesday, July 29. Let's take a look at the week's key events.

Stock Market Earnings Next Week

  • Monday: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), AstraZeneca (AZN), Celestica (CLS)
  • Tuesday: Visa (V), Coca-Cola (KO), Seagate Technology Holdings (STX), Boeing (BA)
  • Wednesday: Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Lam Research (LRCX), Arm (ARM)
  • Thursday: Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), Mastercard (MA), Altria (MO)
  • Friday: ExxonMobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), AbbVie (ABBV)

Economic Data Next Week

data.title: American Express Stock (AXP) Falls 5% on Mixed Financial Results
data.description:
The stock of credit card giant American Express (AXP) is down 5% after the company delivered mixed financial results for this year's second quarter.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The company posted earnings per share of $4.53, which beat Wall Street's forecast of $4.40. However, revenue in the April through June quarter totaled $19.64 billion, slightly below analysts' consensus expectation of $19.69 billion. Still, sales were up 10% from a year earlier.

Overall spending by cardmembers rose 9% during the quarter as American Express' wealthy clientele continued to spend vigorously. Wall Street analysts and traders closely watch American Express' financial results as the company's credit cards are widely used by high-income earners and leading companies.

American Express' Forward Guidance

In terms of its forward guidance, American Express said it now expects full-year revenue growth of 10%, up slightly from a prior outlook of 9% to 10% growth. The company maintained its 2026 profit guidance that calls for earnings per share of $17.30 to $17.90.

American Express CEO Stephen Squeri said in the earnings release that the company saw stronger momentum than expected during this year's first six months. He added that the company has been attracting more new customers, particularly among younger generations.

Despite some positive metrics, AXP stock is sinking on the mixed results and outlook for the remainder of the year.

Is AXP Stock a Buy?

American Express' stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AXP price target of $376.44 implies 16% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Are Chip Stocks MU, SNDK, and AMD Falling Today? — Citi Says Buy the Dip
data.description:
Chip stocks fell on Friday, with Micron (MU), SanDisk (SNDK), and AMD (AMD) among the names trading lower. The drop came amid broader weakness in the chip sector, as investors grew more cautious about the recent AI-led rally. Memory stocks faced added pressure from rising competition from Chinese chipmakers such as CXMT and YMTC. Despite the sell-off, Citi sees the pullback as a buying opportunity, pointing to data centers, which now make up 34% of total chip demand.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

SNDK has a long & short ETF? Explore SNXX & SNDQ

Why Does Citi Say Buy the Dip in Chip Stocks?

Citi says buy the dip because it expects data center chip demand to keep growing. In fact, the bank expects this market to become larger than today's entire chip market by 2030.

Recent earnings also support Citi's bullish view. Among chip firms that have reported so far, Wall Street's 2026 revenue estimates have risen by 4%. Estimates for 2027 have increased by 7%. Earnings estimates are moving higher as well. EPS forecasts for 2026 and 2027 have risen by 7% and 8%, respectively.

Still, Citi prefers chip equipment stocks over chipmakers. The bank believes rising spending on new chip plants and tools could give these firms a bigger boost.

Intel, TSMC, and Tesla Are Spending Billions on Chips

Citi pointed to recent spending plans from Intel (INTC), TSMC (TSM), and Tesla (TSLA) as a good sign for chip equipment firms.

Intel raised its 2026 capital spending forecast to more than $20 billion from about $18 billion. Spending on chipmaking tools is expected to rise 40% from last year. Intel also expects its total spending to rise again in 2027.

At the same time, TSMC raised its 2026 spending forecast to between $60 billion and $64 billion, up from about $56 billion. The chip giant cited stronger-than-expected AI demand as one reason for the increase.

Meanwhile, Tesla also expects to spend more than $25 billion this year. Its spending is set to remain high over the next two to three years, with some of that money going toward chip production. For Citi, the higher spending plans from Intel, TSMC, and Tesla are a good sign for chip equipment firms, which the bank currently prefers over chipmakers.

Wall Street's Take on These Chip Stocks    

According to TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, Nvidia (NVDA), Micron, SanDisk, AMD, Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate (STX) have Strong Buy consensus ratings. Intel has a Hold rating.

Among these stocks, analysts see the most upside in Micron at 65.70%, followed by Nvidia at 46.68% and SanDisk at 34.94%. Intel offers 22.41% upside, while Western Digital and Seagate have 21.50% and 16.10% upside, respectively. Analysts see 5.94% upside in AMD.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Gains With New Supply Chain Hub in Long Island
data.description:
While most are still keeping a watch for exciting new Halloween decorations at home improvement giant Home Depot (HD), Home Depot is working on ways to better get those hotly-desired items to stores. To that end, it is working on a major new supply chain hub on Long Island. The news was welcome to shareholders, who gave Home Depot stock a 2% boost in Friday morning's trading.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The new supply chain hub—a warehouse and distribution center—will be installed in Yaphank, a "hamlet and census-designated place" on Long Island. The building will reportedly measure 414,000 square feet, and construction began a little while back. Once complete, Home Depot will lease the warehouse, and the building's shell should be complete sometime in the fall of 2027.

Once complete, Home Depot will handle renovations inside the shell to get the building to Home Depot's overall standard. Reports noted that the building's total cost will come in around $157 million, and looked for tax exemptions of a comparatively meager $481,250 for building renovations. Reports also suggested that the development should create around 200 new jobs, a boost for Yaphank as a whole.

A "Scandi-Style A-Frame"

If you have been bemoaning the lack of space around your place lately, or if you are anticipating house guests you would rather not see so much, then Home Depot may have an alternative for you. Home Depot is currently selling what reports describe as a "Scandi-style A-frame" that would likely work well as a complete guest house, or perhaps even a small starter home.

The building measures 19 feet by 23 feet, and costs $43,225. For a first home, that is actually pretty decent, assuming you can get the land for it and add in all the plumbing, HVAC and electric connections. The building even offers an external balcony which could be a welcome outdoor connection in the second half of summer going into fall. It is called the "Hud-1 EZ Buildings A-Frame Moose," and might be the solution to several different issues at once.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 18 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 13.49% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $377.95 per share implies 14.23% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Vivakor (VIVK) Up Today?
data.description:
Vivakor (VIVK) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the energy company announced new deals. The company revealed two new recurring crude oil marketing programs through its Vivakor Supply & Trading unit. The agreements are expected to generate about $384 million in annualized commercial activity, bringing the company's announced marketing platform to more than $1.09 billion based on current oil prices. The new contracts begin August 1, 2026, and run through July 31, 2027.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The new programs cover recurring crude oil purchase and sale transactions at the Cushing and Midland terminals. Together, they add about 400,000 barrels of marketed crude each month, or 4.8 million barrels per year. Vivakor said the deals support its strategy of building an integrated marketing business alongside its transportation, terminal, and storage operations.

"Surpassing $1 billion of announced annualized commercial activity marks an important milestone in the continued growth of Vivakor Supply & Trading," said Chairman, President, and CEO James Ballengee. He added that the expanded programs reflect strong customer relationships and continued growth across the company's marketing platform.

Investors will note that the announced commercial activity does not equal revenue or profit. As a physical oil marketer, Vivakor earns only a small portion of the total contract value as gross profit because it acts as an intermediary in the supply chain. Instead, actual financial results will depend on oil prices, market conditions, delivered volumes, and transaction terms.

What This Meant for Vivakor Stock Today

VIVK stock experienced a 33.49% rally on Friday, but was still down 93.47% year-to-date. On that same note, the stock has fallen 99.94% over the past 12 months.

VIVK stock trading activity was elevated today, as some 70 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume was about 5.22 million.

This is the second time that VIVK stock has spiked this week on oil deal news. The other was news that its oil contracts have hit $290 million.

Is Vivakor Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, Vivakor lacks traditional analyst coverage. Instead, traders can rely on TipRanks' AI analyst Spark for further insight into the shares. Using the OpenAI – 5.2 model, Spark rates VIVK stock as Neutral with a $2 price target. It cites "weak financial performance" and "very bearish technicals" as reasons for this stance. (See VIVK Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Business Activity Growth Accelerates on World Cup, July 4 Holiday
data.description:
U.S. business activity growth reached an eight-month high at the beginning of the third quarter, S&P Global (SPGI) said on Friday. The firm's U.S. Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) was 53.6 in July, topping expectations of 51.5 and up from 51.2 in June. A reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 points to contraction.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Within the index, services led the growth as the sector received a boost from the World Cup and the July 4 holiday. "Firms cited a lift from FIFA World Cup-related spending, stronger-than-usual July 4th activity and increased investment in sales, marketing and product development," said S&P Global.

Manufacturing Slips as Costs Continue to Grow

At the same time, manufacturing activity fell due to supply chain delays caused by the U.S.-Iran war. The Manufacturing PMI dropped to a four-month low of 53.8, declining from 53.9 in June. Manufacturers continued stockpiling inventory as a precaution amid price and supply concerns.

Meanwhile, S&P said input cost inflation hit its highest level since May 2025, driven by higher energy prices, shipping costs, and tariffs. In addition, selling price inflation approached a four-year high.
data.author: Eddie Pan
